document.title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits
document.text:
# Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits

## Abstract

We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks. The system robustly generalizes across domains and is the first agentic workflow for scientific claim auditing.

## Results

We compare CappinCheck against a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X. The baseline system achieves 84.1% on the benchmark. CappinCheck achieves 87.3% on the same benchmark.

| System | Benchmark X accuracy |
| --- | ---: |
| Baseline | 84.1% |
| CappinCheck | 87.3% |

## Discussion

The benchmark contains curated technical claims from public documents. We did not evaluate prospective real-world deployment, clinical documents, legal documents, or private enterprise workflows. We did not run an external literature search for prior scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems.

claims.claim: We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks.
claims.why_risky: The results section only shows an improvement from 84.1% to 87.3% on Benchmark X, which is a 3.2% absolute or 3.8% relative improvement. There is no evidence supporting a 30% improvement.
claims.audit_question: What is the mathematical basis for the 30% improvement claim, and does it refer to a metric or baseline not presented in the text?

claims.claim: The system robustly generalizes across domains
claims.why_risky: The authors state in the discussion that they did not evaluate clinical documents, legal documents, or private enterprise workflows, and only tested on Benchmark X.
claims.audit_question: Which specific domains was the system tested on to prove robust generalization, and what were the performance metrics in each domain?

claims.claim: is the first agentic workflow for scientific claim auditing.
claims.why_risky: The discussion section explicitly states that the authors did not run an external literature search for prior scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems, undermining the claim of being the first.
claims.audit_question: Does a comprehensive literature search of prior scientific claim-auditing and fact-checking systems confirm that CappinCheck is indeed the first agentic workflow of its kind?

claims.claim: CappinCheck achieves 87.3% on the same benchmark.
claims.why_risky: Benchmark X details are not provided, and the evaluation lacks statistical significance testing, error bars, or details on potential data leakage.
claims.audit_question: What are the confidence intervals, statistical significance, and split details for CappinCheck's 87.3% accuracy on Benchmark X?

claims.claim: The baseline system achieves 84.1% on the benchmark.
claims.why_risky: The baseline claim-review pipeline is not defined, making it impossible to determine if it represents a state-of-the-art comparison or a weak baseline.
claims.audit_question: What is the exact architecture, model size, and hyperparameter configuration of the baseline system used for comparison?

claims.claim: We compare CappinCheck against a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X.
claims.why_risky: No references or details are provided for Benchmark X, preventing replication and raising questions about whether the benchmark is a standard, un-biased test.
claims.audit_question: Is Benchmark X a standard, peer-reviewed benchmark, and where are its dataset and source code hosted for replication?

claims.claim: The benchmark contains curated technical claims from public documents.
claims.why_risky: Curated public documents might introduce selection bias, and without clear curation criteria, the dataset may not be representative of actual scientific claim auditing.
claims.audit_question: What were the explicit curation guidelines, annotator agreement metrics, and source document types used to construct Benchmark X?

claims.why_risky: The discussion notes that they did not evaluate prospective real-world deployment, indicating the tasks evaluated may be purely offline or synthetic.
claims.audit_question: What constitutes a 'real-world task' in this evaluation, and how does the offline benchmark performance correlate with actual real-world deployment success?

claim.claim: We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks.
claim.why_risky: The results section only shows an improvement from 84.1% to 87.3% on Benchmark X, which is a 3.2% absolute or 3.8% relative improvement. There is no evidence supporting a 30% improvement.
claim.audit_question: What is the mathematical basis for the 30% improvement claim, and does it refer to a metric or baseline not presented in the text?

audits.why: The claim of a 30% performance improvement is mathematically contradicted by the paper's results, which show only a 3.2% absolute (3.8% relative) improvement on Benchmark X (87.3% vs 84.1%). Furthermore, the assertion of evaluating on 'real-world tasks' and improving over 'prior work' is directly contradicted by the authors' admissions that they did not evaluate prospective real-world deployments and did not conduct an external literature search for prior work.
audits.weaker_supported_rewrite: We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 3.2% absolute (3.8% relative) over a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X.

counter_evidence.source_title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits
counter_evidence.snippet: We compare CappinCheck against a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X. The baseline system achieves 84.1% on the benchmark. CappinCheck achieves 87.3% on the same benchmark.
counter_evidence.relevance: Provides the actual performance comparison, showing a 3.2% absolute or 3.8% relative improvement, which directly contradicts the claimed 30%.

counter_evidence.source_title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits - Discussion
counter_evidence.snippet: The benchmark contains curated technical claims from public documents. We did not evaluate prospective real-world deployment, clinical documents, legal documents, or private enterprise workflows.
counter_evidence.relevance: Directly contradicts the claim that the system was tested on real-world tasks.

counter_evidence.source_title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits - Discussion
counter_evidence.snippet: We did not run an external literature search for prior scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems.
counter_evidence.relevance: Undermines the claim of improving over prior work as no literature search was performed to verify prior art.

missing_context: The mathematical basis for the 30% improvement claim is completely absent from the text.
No evaluation or metrics for real-world tasks other than Benchmark X are presented.
The text lacks any explanation of what prior work was compared against, other than a single baseline pipeline.
